name: Dispute Resolution — Negotiation, Mediation and Arbitration
text: In the event of any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, the Parties shall first attempt to resolve the dispute amicably through good-faith negotiations for a period of fifteen (15) days. If the dispute is not resolved within that period, the dispute shall be referred to arbitration in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The arbitration shall be conducted by a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by the Parties, and if the Parties fail to agree on the appointment within fifteen (15) days, the arbitrator shall be appointed in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The seat and venue of arbitration shall be {{arbitration_city}}, India. The arbitration proceedings shall be conducted in the English language. Subject to the arbitration clause, the courts in {{arbitration_city}} shall have supervisory jurisdiction.

legal_basis.note: Arbitration does not oust courts — valid exception

invalid_if: Dispute resolution mechanism is entirely absent
Clause omits the seat of arbitration
Clause omits any arbitrator appointment mechanism
Clause restricts access to courts absolutely
source: Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996; Mediation Act, 2023
title: Dispute Resolution Mechanism

jurisdiction_constraints.general: Seat determines supervisory court — pick high court city (Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad)
jurisdiction_constraints.Maharashtra: Consider MCIA rules — Mumbai seat; MHCA High Court supervises
jurisdiction_constraints.Delhi: DIAC rules available; Delhi High Court supervises

dispute_hotspots: Missing seat — Bhatia International conflict now resolved by BALCO but drafting ambiguity remains
Time limits in arbitration clause — courts have struck down very short limitation periods
Named institutional arbitrator dissolved or renamed
